columns.extraction_plan: In the 'abstract' section, under 'METHODS', it states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' In the 'INTRODUCTION' section, it further clarifies: 'In ARASENS, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to double-blind treatment with either darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.'

From Table 1 (Chunk 1, page 5), the column headers for the placebo group are consistently 'Placebo (n = X)'. The full treatment is described in the table title as 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel'. Therefore, the control arm is 'Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel'.
In Table 1, locate the "All Patients" column group. Under "Darolutamide (n = 652)", find the row "Age, median, years (range)". The median age is "67.0".
In Table 1, locate the "All Patients" column group. Under "Placebo (n = 650)", find the row "Age, median, years (range)". The median age is "67.0".

Reviewed abstract, methods, results, discussion sections (Chunks 8, 9, 10), appendix tables and figures (Chunks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 12). The document is an original research report of a randomized controlled trial (ARASENS), not a systematic review or meta-analysis that would provide an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence across multiple randomized controlled trials. The paper focuses on reporting the efficacy and safety outcomes of the ARASENS trial itself.

On page 2, in the "METHODS" section under "Trial Design", it states: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." This indicates that docetaxel was part of the allocated treatment for all patients in the darolutamide arm. Figure 1 on page 3, in the "CONSORT flow diagram", shows that for the "Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT" arm, "Received allocated treatment (n = 651)". Therefore, 651 patients (100% of those who received allocated treatment) in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

On page 2, in the "METHODS" section under "Trial Design", it states: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." This indicates that docetaxel was part of the allocated treatment for all patients in the placebo arm. Figure 1 on page 3, in the "CONSORT flow diagram", shows that for the "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT" arm, "Received allocated treatment (n = 651)". Therefore, 651 patients (100% of those who received allocated treatment) in the control arm received docetaxel.
Extract the value "NCT02799602" from the "CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION" section on page 12.
I examined the header and footer of page 1, the "ASSOCIATED CONTENT" section on page 1, and the "CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION" section on page 12. No PubMed ID was found.

David Crawford, MD; Neal Shore, MD; Evgeny Kopyltsov, MD; Arash Rezazadeh Kalebasty, MD; Martin Bögemann, MD; Dingwei Ye, MD; Felipe Cruz, MD, PhD; Hiroyoshi Suzuki, MD, PhD; Shivani Kapur, MD; Shankar Srinivasan, PhD; Frank Verholen, MD; Iris Kuss, MD; Heikki Joensuu, MD; and Matthew R. Smith, MD, PhD".
Extract "2023" from the copyright information "© 2023 by American Society of Clinical Oncology" at the bottom of page 1.

The document is a multi-page PDF (15 pages of content) with detailed methods, results, figures, and tables, indicating a full publication rather than just an abstract. The title also includes "original reports" on page 1.

Extract "Phase III" from the title "Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial" on page 1.

The document is labeled "original reports" on page 1. The abstract and introduction describe the study as evaluating efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial, and the "Knowledge Generated" section on page 2 states "These subgroup analyses of ARASENS showed...". This indicates it's an original report of subgroup analyses from the ARASENS trial.
In Figure 1, under "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT", locate "Analyzed for primary end point". The value is n = 654.

The 'Median Follow-Up Duration (mo)' is not reported in the PDF. The document mentions 'median treatment duration' in the 'Patient Characteristics' section (Chunk 9, page 4) and the 'Discussion' section (Chunk 10, page 11), stating, for example, 'median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel versus 34.1 months in the docetaxel subgroup of the abiraterone group in PEACE-1'. However, this refers to the duration patients received treatment, not the total follow-up duration for the study. I also reviewed the abstract (Chunk 8, page 1), the 'Methods' section (Chunk 8, page 2), Table 1 (Chunk 1, page 5), Table 2 (Chunk 4, page 8), Table 3 (Chunk 5, page 9), Table 4 (Chunk 6, page 10), Figure 2 (Chunk 2, page 6), Figure 3 (Chunk 3, page 7), and Figure A1 (Chunk 7, page 16), and did not find the median follow-up duration.
In the "DISCUSSION" section, first paragraph, first column, the text states: "median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel".

I examined the "abstract" (Chunk 8, page 1), "METHODS" (Chunk 8, page 2-3), "RESULTS" section, specifically the "Patient Characteristics" paragraph (Chunk 9, page 4), and the "DISCUSSION" section (Chunk 10, page 11). While subgroup-specific median durations for the placebo arm are mentioned (e.g., 15.3 months for high-volume, 26.5 months for low-volume on page 4), an overall median on-treatment duration for the placebo arm of the ARASENS trial is not reported. The 34.1 months mentioned on page 11 refers to a subgroup in the PEACE-1 trial, not the ARASENS placebo arm.

columns.extraction_plan: The 'Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1' is not explicitly listed in the PDF. However, the text describes the agents in Treatment Arm 1 as darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT), and docetaxel. The classes of these agents are inferred from the text, with 'Darolutamide' classified as an Androgen receptor pathway inhibitor, 'ADT' as Androgen-deprivation therapy, and 'Docetaxel' as Chemotherapy. These classifications are based on the 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1, paragraph 1, and references to 'chemohormonal therapy' in the context of docetaxel.

This indicates that an aspect of quality of life (disease-related physical symptoms) was assessed and reported.

Abstract (Chunk 8, page 1), the "RESULTS" section (Chunk 8, page 3, and Chunk 9, pages 4-7), Table 1 (Chunk 1, page 5), Table 2 (Chunk 4, page 8), Table 3 (Chunk 5, page 9), Table 4 (Chunk 6, page 10), and Figure 2 (Chunk 2, page 6) and Figure 3 (Chunk 3, page 7) were examined. The study used the "Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire" (FACT-P), which is a Quality of Life Scale. This is mentioned in the "Study Assessments" section on page 3 (Chunk 8). A specific endpoint derived from this scale, "Time to worsening disease-related symptoms," is reported in Table 2, "Secondary Efficacy End Points for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS," on page 8 (Chunk 4). For this endpoint, the table provides the median time in months, the number and percentage of patients with an event, and the Hazard Ratio (HR) with 95% Confidence Interval (CI) for various subgroups and for all patients. For example, for "All Patients - Darolutamide (n = 651)", the median time is 19.3 months, 351 (54%) patients had an event, and the HR (95% CI) is 1.04 (0.89 to 1.22).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients with synchronous metastases in the Darolutamide treatment arm can be calculated by summing the "De novo mHSPC" counts from the "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis" row in Table 1 (page 5) for the Darolutamide High Volume subgroup (432 patients) and the Darolutamide Low Volume subgroup (126 patients). This totals 432 + 126 = 558 patients. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (Figure 1, page 3). Therefore, the percentage is (558 / 651) * 100 = 85.7%.
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients with synchronous metastases in the Placebo control arm can be calculated by summing the "De novo mHSPC" counts from the "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis" row in Table 1 (page 5) for the Placebo High Volume subgroup (445 patients) and the Placebo Low Volume subgroup (121 patients). This totals 445 + 121 = 566 patients. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (Figure 1, page 3). Therefore, the percentage is (566 / 654) * 100 = 86.5%.
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients with metachronous metastases in the Darolutamide treatment arm can be calculated by summing the "Recurrent mHSPC" counts from the "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis" row in Table 1 (page 5) for the Darolutamide High Volume subgroup (58 patients) and the Darolutamide Low Volume subgroup (28 patients). This totals 58 + 28 = 86 patients. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (Figure 1, page 3). Therefore, the percentage is (86 / 651) * 100 = 13.2%.
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients with metachronous metastases in the Placebo control arm can be calculated by summing the "Recurrent mHSPC" counts from the "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis" row in Table 1 (page 5) for the Placebo High Volume subgroup (59 patients) and the Placebo Low Volume subgroup (23 patients). This totals 59 + 23 = 82 patients. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (Figure 1, page 3). Therefore, the percentage is (82 / 654) * 100 = 12.5%.

Extract the regimen for the experimental arm. The abstract states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The Methods section on page 2 further specifies: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 Regimen is: "oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily in combination with ADT and docetaxel".
Extract the name of the experimental arm as "Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel" from Table 1 (Chunk 1, page 5) column headers.

In Table 1, locate the row "ECOG PS, No. (%)" and the sub-row "0". Sum the values from the "Darolutamide (n = 497)" column under "High Volume" and the "Darolutamide (n = 154)" column under "Low Volume". High Volume - Darolutamide: 343, Low Volume - Darolutamide: 123. Total Darolutamide PS 0 = 343 + 123 = 466. Total Darolutamide patients = 497 (High Volume) + 154 (Low Volume) = 651. Percentage = (466 / 651) * 100 = 71.58%.

In Table 1, locate the row "ECOG PS, No. (%)" and the sub-row "0". Sum the values from the "Placebo (n = 508)" column under "High Volume" and the "Placebo (n = 146)" column under "Low Volume". High Volume - Placebo: 349, Low Volume - Placebo: 113. Total Placebo PS 0 = 349 + 113 = 462. Total Placebo patients = 508 (High Volume) + 146 (Low Volume) = 654. Percentage = (462 / 654) * 100 = 70.64%.

Table 1 (page 5) provides ECOG PS distribution for PS 0 and PS 1, but does not explicitly report PS 2. The footnote states "ECOG PS scores range from 0 to 5", and the sum of patients with PS 0, PS 1, and "Missing" accounts for all patients in the Darolutamide arm (466 + 185 + 0 = 651). Therefore, PS 2 is not reported as a distinct category, and a value for the range "1-2" cannot be provided. The value for PS 1 in the Darolutamide arm is 185 (28.4%).

Table 1 (page 5) provides ECOG PS distribution for PS 0 and PS 1, but does not explicitly report PS 2. The footnote states "ECOG PS scores range from 0 to 5", and the sum of patients with PS 0, PS 1, and "Missing" accounts for all patients in the Placebo arm (462 + 190 + 2 = 654). Therefore, PS 2 is not reported as a distinct category, and a value for the range "1-2" cannot be provided. The value for PS 1 in the Placebo arm is 190 (29.1%).

Table 1 (page 5) provides categories such as "M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone". While liver metastases would fall under "visceral metastases", the table does not provide a specific breakdown for liver involvement. Abstract and methods sections (Chunk 8, pages 1-4) define "high-volume disease" and "high-risk disease" by the presence of "visceral metastases" but do not specify individual organs like the liver.

Table 1 (page 5) provides categories such as "M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone". Abstract and methods sections (Chunk 8, pages 1-4) define "high-volume disease" and "high-risk disease" by the presence of "visceral metastases" but do not specify individual organs like the lungs.

For the Darolutamide arm, sum the counts and total patients from the "High Volume - Darolutamide (n = 497)" and "Low Volume - Darolutamide (n = 154)" columns for the "M1b, bone metastases ± LN" row. High Volume - Darolutamide: 386 patients. Low Volume - Darolutamide: 131 patients. Total Darolutamide patients: 497 + 154 = 651. Total patients with bone metastases (M1b) in Darolutamide arm: 386 + 131 = 517. Percentage: (517 / 651) * 100 = 79.4%. Value: 517 (79.4%)

For the Placebo arm, sum the counts and total patients from the "High Volume - Placebo (n = 508)" and "Low Volume - Placebo (n = 146)" columns for the "M1b, bone metastases ± LN" row. High Volume - Placebo: 390 patients. Low Volume - Placebo: 131 patients. Total Placebo patients: 508 + 146 = 654. Total patients with bone metastases (M1b) in Placebo arm: 390 + 131 = 521. Percentage: (521 / 654) * 100 = 79.7%. Value: 521 (79.7%)

Table 1 (page 5) provides "M1a, nonregional LN only" and also indicates "± LN" in "M1b, bone metastases ± LN" and "M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone". While M1a specifically refers to nodal metastases, the "± LN" in other categories means that patients with bone or visceral metastases could also have nodal involvement. The table does not provide a consolidated count or percentage for *all* patients with any nodal metastases across all categories. Therefore, the total N (%) for "Nodal" metastases cannot be accurately determined from the provided data.

Table 1 (page 5) provides "M1a, nonregional LN only" and also indicates "± LN" in "M1b, bone metastases ± LN" and "M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone". While M1a specifically refers to nodal metastases, the "± LN" in other categories means that patients with bone or visceral metastases could also have nodal involvement. The table does not provide a consolidated count or percentage for *all* patients with any nodal metastases across all categories. Therefore, the total N (%) for "Nodal" metastases cannot be accurately determined from the provided data.

The abstract on page 1 states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The "INTRODUCTION" section on page 1 refers to this as "early treatment intensification with a combination of androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus either docetaxel or an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor". The "Relevance" section on page 2 explicitly calls it "Triplet therapy". Therefore, the treatment class can be categorized as **Triplet therapy (Androgen receptor pathway inhibitor + ADT + Docetaxel)**.

From the "abstract" section on page 1 (Chunk 8), extract: "darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel". From the "METHODS" section on page 2 (Chunk 8), extract: "oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel". From Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3, the treatment arms are labeled: "Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT" and "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT". From Table 1 (Chunk 1) on page 5, the table title refers to "Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel". From Figure 2 (Chunk 2) on page 6 and Figure 3 (Chunk 3) on page 7, the curve labels specify: "Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel" and "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel".

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states: 'Timing of metastatic presentation and disease volume and risk have been used to assess prognosis in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.11-14 The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease).' The 'RESULTS' section on page 4 also mentions: 'The effect of darolutamide treatment on OS across prespecified subgroups, including de novo and recurrent disease, was favorable across all subgroups of patients with high-volume and almost all subgroups with low-volume disease (Appendix Fig A1, online only).' Table 1 on page 5, under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', provides data for 'De novo mHSPC' (which is synonymous with synchronous disease). Figure A1 on page 16, under 'Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis', presents OS data for the 'De novo' subgroup.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states: 'Timing of metastatic presentation and disease volume and risk have been used to assess prognosis in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.11-14 The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease).' The 'RESULTS' section on page 4 also mentions: 'The effect of darolutamide treatment on OS across prespecified subgroups, including de novo and recurrent disease, was favorable across all subgroups of patients with high-volume and almost all subgroups with low-volume disease (Appendix Fig A1, online only).' Table 1 on page 5, under 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)', provides data for 'Recurrent mHSPC' (which is synonymous with metachronous disease). Figure A1 on page 16, under 'Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis', presents OS data for the 'Recurrent' subgroup.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: The 'abstract' on page 1 explicitly states: 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82).' The 'METHODS' section on page 2 defines 'High-volume disease' and states that subgroups were assessed by disease volume. Table 1 on page 5, titled 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS', includes 'High Volume' as a primary column header. Figure 2 (A) on page 6 shows OS for 'high-volume disease'. Figure 3 (A) on page 7 shows time to CRPC for 'high-volume disease'. Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, and Table 4 on page 10 all include 'High Volume' as a primary column header for secondary efficacy endpoints and adverse events. Figure A1 (A) on page 16 also presents OS data for 'high-volume disease'.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: The 'abstract' on page 1 explicitly states: 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo... in low-risk disease (HR, 0.62; 95% CI, 0.42 to 0.90), and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).' The 'METHODS' section on page 2 defines 'Low-volume and low-risk disease' and states that subgroups were assessed by disease volume. Table 1 on page 5, titled 'Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS', includes 'Low Volume' as a primary column header. Figure 2 (B) on page 6 shows OS for 'low-volume disease'. Figure 3 (B) on page 7 shows time to CRPC for 'low-volume disease'. Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, and Table 4 on page 10 all include 'Low Volume' as a primary column header for secondary efficacy endpoints and adverse events. Figure A1 (B) on page 17 also presents OS data for 'low-volume disease'.
